therapist (instead of an assistant) must assess the patient’s function using a 
method which objectively measures activities of daily living such as, but 
not limited to, eating, swallowing, bathing, dressing, toileting, walking, 
climbing stairs, using assistive devices, and mental and cognitive factors. 
The measurement results must be documented in the clinical record.
